name: Suggested Edit
description: Standard for the propose→accept/reject lifecycle of a collaborative suggested edit — the genuinely hard, recurring, unowned mutation that rides on a suggestion-motivation annotation until a viewer accepts or rejects it. Grounded in WAI-ARIA 1.3 role=suggestion (editor’s draft Feb 2026), which mints the role, scopes it to editable documents, and wraps one insertion and one deletion inside the suggestion element. The SuggestedEdit interface is the state machine both consumers compose: annotation as the suggestion motivation’s body; the Editor Engine protocol as the transaction to apply (present when the host is editable; record-only over read-only hosts — PDF / foreign HTML / no engine). The target field composes the Durable Range Anchor contract (#1471) — this contract owns the what (lifecycle state + payload); range-anchor owns the where (re-resolvable durable location). Carve-out by #2029 (ratified 2026-07-02, the #intents-ux-only corollary applied again); authored in #2145. The contract is the only lock; apply dispatch, CRDT integration, and optimistic-revert are impl (Frontier UI).
summary: The propose→accept/reject lifecycle for a collaborative suggested edit — composed by annotation (suggestion body) and the Editor Engine protocol (apply transaction).
